init.description: Create an empty Git repository or reinitialize an existing one
init.completeDescription: Initializes a new, empty Git repository in the current directory, creating a .git folder.

clone.description: Clone a repository into a new directory
clone.completeDescription: Downloads an existing Git repository from a remote source along with all its history and branches.

--depth.description: Create a shallow clone with a truncated history
--depth.completeDescription: Clones the repository but limits the history to the specified number of commits, saving bandwidth.

add.description: Add file contents to the index
add.completeDescription: Stages modified or new files, preparing them to be included in the next commit.

..description: Stage all changes in the current directory
..completeDescription: Stages all new, modified, and deleted files in the current directory and its subdirectories.

-p.description: Interactives choose hunks of patch between the index and the work tree
-p.completeDescription: Allows you to interactively review and select specific changes within files to stage.

status.completeDescription: Displays the state of the working directory and the staging area, showing which changes have been staged, which haven't, and which files aren't being tracked.

-s.description: Give the output in the short-format
-s.completeDescription: Provides a highly condensed, short-format output of the repository status.

commit.completeDescription: The git commit command captures a snapshot of the project's currently staged changes. It allows you to save your work and create a history of changes in the repository.

-m.completeDescription: Sets the commit message for the commit

-a.description: Automatically stage files that have been modified
-a.completeDescription: Automatically stages all modified files before committing

--amend.description: Replace the tip of the current branch by creating a new commit
--amend.completeDescription: Allows you to modify the most recent commit

log.completeDescription: Displays the commit history of the current branch, showing commit hashes, authors, dates, and messages.

--oneline.description: Print each commit in a single line
--oneline.completeDescription: Condenses the commit history output, displaying only the short SHA-1 and the commit message line.

--graph.description: Draw a text-based graphical representation of the commit history
--graph.completeDescription: Draws an ASCII graph representing the branch and merge history.

checkout.description: Switch branches or restore working tree files
checkout.completeDescription: Switches between branches or restores working tree files

-b.description: Create and checkout a new branch
-b.completeDescription: Creates a new branch and switches to it

branch.completeDescription: Manages branches within your local repository. Without arguments, it lists all local branches.

-d.completeDescription: Deletes the specified branch only if it has already been fully merged into its upstream branch.

-D.completeDescription: Forces the deletion of a branch, even if it contains unmerged changes.

-a.description: List both remote-tracking and local branches
-a.completeDescription: Shows a list of all local and remote branches available to your local Git environment.

merge.description: Join two or more development histories together
merge.completeDescription: Integrates changes from another branch into your current active branch.

--no-ff.description: Create a merge commit even when a fast-forward is possible
--no-ff.completeDescription: Forces Git to create a new merge commit instead of fast-forwarding, preserving the historical branch existence.

--abort.description: Abort the current conflict resolution process
--abort.completeDescription: Resets the merge process and tries to reconstruct the pre-merge state if a conflict occurs.

pull.description: Fetch from and integrate with another repository or a local branch
pull.completeDescription: Incorporates changes from a remote repository into the current branch. It is a combination of 'git fetch' followed by 'git merge'.

--rebase.description: Rebase the current branch on top of the upstream branch after fetching
--rebase.completeDescription: Fetches the remote changes and replays your local commits on top of them, avoiding unnecessary merge commits.

push.description: Update remote refs along with associated objects
push.completeDescription: Updates remote references with the local changes

-u.completeDescription: Links your local branch to a remote branch, so you can use 'git push' or 'git pull' without specifying the destination in the future.

--force.description: Force push changes to the remote repository
--force.completeDescription: Overrides the remote branch with your local history. Use with extreme caution as it can overwrite others' work.

fetch.description: Download objects and refs from another repository
fetch.completeDescription: Downloads metadata and commits from a remote repository, but does not integrate or merge them into your working files.

--all.completeDescription: Fetches tracking updates from all configured remote repositories.

stash.description: Stash the changes in a dirty working directory away
stash.completeDescription: Temporarily shelves (stores) changes you've made to your working copy so you can work on something else, and then come back and re-apply them later.

pop.description: Remove a single stashed state from the stash list and apply it
pop.completeDescription: Applies the most recently saved stash changes to your working directory and removes it from the stash list.

list.description: List the stash entries that you currently have
list.completeDescription: Displays all currently saved stashes in the repository.

apply.description: Apply the stash without removing it from the list
apply.completeDescription: Applies the most recently saved stash changes but keeps the data stored in the stash list.
